title: 'Disruptive' passenger restrained on Jet2 flight to Manchester dies

input.lead: A man who was restrained on a Jet2 flight by fellow travellers after he allegedly assaulted another passenger and cabin crew, has died Police were called to reports of a passenger attacking another customer and a staff member on the Jet2 flight from Cyprus to Manchester and being
input.summary: A man who was restrained on a Jet2 flight by fellow travellers after he allegedly assaulted another passenger and cabin crew, has died Police were called to reports of a passenger attacking another customer and a staff member on the Jet2 flight from Cyprus to Manchester and being
input.evidence_text: A man who was restrained on a Jet2 flight by fellow travellers after he allegedly assaulted another passenger and cabin crew, has died Police were called to reports of a passenger attacking another customer and a staff member on the Jet2 flight from Cyprus to Manchester and being "disruptive and aggressive" in the early hours of Monday On landing at Manchester Airport, officers used handcuffs to detain Callum Kerr, 35, of Warrington, but quickly found he was "unresponsive" and performed CPR on him before taking him to hospital, Greater Manchester Police (GMP) said Kerr, a bare-knuckle fighter, died on Tuesday. An investigation into his death is under way Paying tribute, Kerr's loved ones said: "Callum was a family man, father to three beautiful young children, a lover of sport and with a heart of gold." GMP said it had made a mandatory referral to the Independent Office for Police Conduct (IOPC) due to police coming into contact with Kerr prior to his death

title: Salford Lads Club made famous by The Smiths gets listed upgrade

input.summary: A "remarkable" 123-year-old youth club building made famous after The Smiths posed outside it for a photo shoot has had its listed status upgraded to Grade II* Salford Lads Club opened on Coronation Street in the city in 1903 and has helped generations of boys - and later girls
input.evidence_text: A "remarkable" 123-year-old youth club building made famous after The Smiths posed outside it for a photo shoot has had its listed status upgraded to Grade II* Salford Lads Club opened on Coronation Street in the city in 1903 and has helped generations of boys - and later girls - by offering activities such as sport, art and music The upgrade came less than two years after a campaign to save it from closure raised £250,000, which included donations from former Smiths singer Morrissey and music legend and former club member Graham Nash Leslie Holmes, culture and heritage projects manager at the club, said it was what he had been working towards "since I first came into the building in 2002" The Salford club was designed by architect Henry Lord and established in 1903, with its official opening happening in January 1904, and it first received Grade II listed status in 2003 It was initially part of a federation of lads clubs in working class communities across the UK, but the majority of the others closed between World War One and World War Two

title: Firms' adverts disguised as parking tickets banned

input.lead: The Advertising Standards Authority said two adverts caused "unjustifiable distress"
input.summary: The Advertising Standards Authority said two adverts caused "unjustifiable distress"
input.evidence_text: Firms' adverts disguised as parking tickets banned - BBC News Image source, ASA Image caption, Parc and Golf Fang both used mock-up parking tickets to entice customers By Dan Wareing North West Published 25 June 2026, 06:06 BST Two companies' promotional fliers that were designed to look like parking tickets have been banned by the advertising watchdog. The Advertising Standards Authority (ASA) said it had upheld complaints made about leaflets from the indoor golf venue Golf Fang Liverpool, and Parc, a car parking app. ASA ruled the adverts, which were all wrapped in yellow and black plastic and placed on windscreens in Liverpool and Manchester, risked causing "unjustifiable distress". Golf Fang subsequently said it would not re-run what it described as "a short promotional campaign". Parc apologised and said it would make sure future campaigns were "obviously identifiable" as adverts from the outset ASA said the Golf Fang leaflet was first seen in April and featured the words "PENALTY CHARGE NOTICED ENCLOSED", and "WARNING, it is an offence for any person other than the driver to remove this notice". But inside the flier was an assurance that the driver had not been fined, and instead listed a link to the company's website. Image source, ASA Image caption, Golf Fang's advert contained a link to their website inside Parc's flyer was reported in February and March, and featured a similar message inside apologising for giving the recipient "a scare". "This isn't a real parking fine, but if you did actually get one today, that's exactly the kind of stress we're trying to stop," it said. title: What is the Greater Manchester mayor and what do they do?

input.lead: Greater Manchester goes to the polls on 30 July, but what powers does the region's mayor hold?
input.summary: Greater Manchester goes to the polls on 30 July, but what powers does the region's mayor hold?
input.evidence_text: Andy Burnham's victory in the Makerfield by-election means he has to step down as Greater Manchester mayor Burnham has been mayor since the role was first created in 2017 and the race to become his successor will be fiercely contested by all involved But what is the role they will be fighting for? The role of mayor was created as part of a law passed in 2016, one of a number of big pieces of devolution legislation brought in by the Conservatives after taking power in 2010 In a 2012 referendum, Manchester rejected having a directly-elected city mayor, along with eight other major cities, but the decision to have a regional or metropolitan mayor was directly negotiated between the government and the 10 Greater Manchester councils Andy Burnham, who left Parliament to stand for the role, was chosen as Labour's candidate for the first mayoral election in 2017 and won, taking 63.4% of the vote

title: How do I register to vote in the May 2026 elections?

input.lead: Here’s what you need to know about how you register to vote and when
input.summary: Here’s what you need to know about how you register to vote and when
input.evidence_text: Click to expand How do I register to vote? Millions of voters across England, Scotland and Wales are heading to the polls on 7 May. Depending where you are, you might be voting to elect your local council members, a new mayor, members of the Welsh Senedd, or Scottish Parliament But if you're going to vote, you need to make sure you are registered. The deadline for registering to vote has passed but there's still time to apply for a postal or proxy vote Here's what you need to know about how and when you register to vote Registering only takes a few minutes if you do it online. It’s the same process whether you plan to vote in person at a polling station, by post, or through a proxy voter Go to the gov.uk website and fill out the form. You'll need your National Insurance number and current address details to hand If you can't provide your NI number you can still complete the online form but you'll need to provide documents like a passport or utility bill to your local electoral registration office before you're properly registered

title: Driver killed man in Wigan after failing to defrost windscreen

input.lead: A woman who repeatedly drove over a man lying in a road after failing to defrost her car windscreen during a winter's morning has been jailed for six years Megan Murphy, 26, pleaded guilty to causing the death of 31-year-old Darryl Tomlinson by dangerous driving.
input.summary: A woman who repeatedly drove over a man lying in a road after failing to defrost her car windscreen during a winter's morning has been jailed for six years Megan Murphy, 26, pleaded guilty to causing the death of 31-year-old Darryl Tomlinson by dangerous driving. He had collapsed
input.evidence_text: A woman who repeatedly drove over a man lying in a road after failing to defrost her car windscreen during a winter's morning has been jailed for six years Megan Murphy, 26, pleaded guilty to causing the death of 31-year-old Darryl Tomlinson by dangerous driving. He had collapsed on Pembroke Road, Wigan, in the hours before he was hit by the car on 9 January 2025 Witnesses tried to alert Murphy as she drove towards Tomlinson, with one shouting "stop" and "go back" and another sounding a car horn Bolton Crown Court heard Murphy later called a friend and said: "I've ran him over but it's a smackhead." Murphy, of Lord Street, Wigan, had got into her car at about 05:47 GMT on the icy morning and reversed from an alleyway, facing the wrong way down the one-way street, Greater Manchester Police said CCTV showed her windscreen and windows were visibly frozen, significantly obstructing her view of the road

title: Mum fears deaf son is missing out at school due to BSL gap

input.lead: The mother of a 10-year-old boy who is profoundly deaf fears her son is missing out on his education due to a lack of teaching staff with British Sign Language (BSL) Caroline, from Blackley in Manchester, believes the current situation is leaving her son Thomas - who is also visu
input.summary: The mother of a 10-year-old boy who is profoundly deaf fears her son is missing out on his education due to a lack of teaching staff with British Sign Language (BSL) Caroline, from Blackley in Manchester, believes the current situation is leaving her son Thomas - who is also visu
input.evidence_text: The mother of a 10-year-old boy who is profoundly deaf fears her son is missing out on his education due to a lack of teaching staff with British Sign Language (BSL) Caroline, from Blackley in Manchester, believes the current situation is leaving her son Thomas - who is also visually impaired - isolated and she is concerned he is "not learning anything meaningful" "Many people assume all special schools can meet every disabled child's needs, but really when your child has a multi-sensory impairment (MSI) it's like a postcode lottery," she said A statement from Manchester City Council, on behalf of the school, said it worked to ensure the "best offer and outcomes for all of our children" A Freedom of Information request by the charity Sense found 65% of local authorities in the north-west of England did not employ a single specialist teacher to support deafblind children Deafblindness is defined by the Deadfblind UK charity as "the loss of sight and hearing to the point where your communication, mobility and ability to access information are impacted"

He represented England three times Bibey's career was cut short by a devastating achilles injury in 2012 and - like so many sporting stars before him - he struggled to replace what professional sport had given him. And at Wednesday’s meeting, the Reform group only entered the council chamber after the prayers were finishe Coun Jones told the meeting: “This is a place of business not a place for praying. We fully respect people’s rights to their own religion and also the right to have no religion. input.summary: Wigan The rise and tragic fall of Ricky Bibey His happy childhood, dramatic sporting rise and tragic fall were laid bare during an inquest into his death View 5 Images Ricky Bibey (Image: MEN) News John Scheerhout Crime reporter 18:03, 25 Jun 2026 Updated 18:36, 25 Jun 2026 It wa
input.evidence_text: Wigan The rise and tragic fall of Ricky Bibey His happy childhood, dramatic sporting rise and tragic fall were laid bare during an inquest into his death View 5 Images Ricky Bibey (Image: MEN) News John Scheerhout Crime reporter 18:03, 25 Jun 2026 Updated 18:36, 25 Jun 2026 It was obvious even from his early days at St Philip's CofE primary school in Atherton that Ricky Bibey was a sporting natural. He excelled at football and swimming, but his passion was rugby His natural flare for the game saw him sign for Wigan Warriors aged just 14, going on to a stellar career playing also for Leigh Centurions, St Helens and Wakefield Trinity during which he won two Challenge Cup winners medals. He represented England three times Bibey's career was cut short by a devastating achilles injury in 2012 and - like so many sporting stars before him - he struggled to replace what professional sport had given him. He became a successful entrepreneur, with a portfolio of rental properties and a window cleaning bus Yet behind all those achievements, and away from the public glare, in the last 10 years of his life Bibey was sinking into depression, fuelled by addiction to booze and drugs. By 2022, at the age of 48, he was dead Bibey's happy childhood, dramatic sporting rise and tragic fall were all laid bare during an inquest into his death at Rochdale Coroners' Court last week

input.evidence_text: Wigan The rise and tragic fall of Ricky Bibey His happy childhood, dramatic sporting rise and tragic fall were laid bare during an inquest into his death View 5 Images Ricky Bibey (Image: MEN) News John Scheerhout Crime reporter 18:03, 25 Jun 2026 Updated 18:36, 25 Jun 2026 It was obvious even from his early days at St Philip's CofE primary school in Atherton that Ricky Bibey was a sporting natural. He excelled at football and swimming, but his passion was rugby His natural flare for the game saw him sign for Wigan Warriors aged just 14, going on to a stellar career playing also for Leigh Centurions, St Helens and Wakefield Trinity during which he won two Challenge Cup winners medals. He represented England three times Bibey's career was cut short by a devastating achilles injury in 2012 and - like so many sporting stars before him - he struggled to replace what professional sport had given him. He became a successful entrepreneur, with a portfolio of rental properties and a window cleaning bus Yet behind all those achievements, and away from the public glare, in the last 10 years of his life Bibey was sinking into depression, fuelled by addiction to booze and drugs. By 2022, at the age of 48, he was dead Bibey's happy childhood, dramatic sporting rise and tragic fall were all laid bare during an inquest into his death at Rochdale Coroners' Court last week

'Frustrated' Eric Mwangi, 35, carried out the crime spree earlier this year. Minshull Street Crown Court heard he was due to be released from prison on Wednesday (June 24) Prosecuting, Amy Weir said Mwangi struck at a car park on Pomona Strand in Trafford . Windscreens were smashed with a metal bar. Ms Weir said almost £4,000-worth of damage was caused. The incident happened at around 11.45pm on March 2 this year Mwangi pleaded guilty to 15 counts of criminal damage. The court heard how he has 11 previous convictions covering 28 offences, including other offences of criminal damage "This appears to be a revenge attack of sorts," Ms Weir said. Defending, Samrat Sengupta described the incident as a 'frustrated rampage'. He said the defendant was experiencing mental health issues and homelessness at the time Sentencing, Judge Jason MacAdam said he intended to pass a sentence that would mean Mwangi would be released from prison with no more time to serve. Mr Sengupta did not offer any further mitigation following the indication from the judge

title: Andy Burnham wins Makerfield by-election: What happens next with Greater Manchester mayoralty?

input.lead: Friday 19 June 2026 at 1:43pm Andy Burnham has been mayor of Greater Manchester for almost a decade.
input.summary: Friday 19 June 2026 at 1:43pm Andy Burnham has been mayor of Greater Manchester for almost a decade. Credit: PA Images Andy Burnham’s historic victory in the Makerfield by-election sets off a rapid chain reaction for both Greater Manchester and the wider UK political landscape A
input.evidence_text: Friday 19 June 2026 at 1:43pm Andy Burnham has been mayor of Greater Manchester for almost a decade. Credit: PA Images Andy Burnham’s historic victory in the Makerfield by-election sets off a rapid chain reaction for both Greater Manchester and the wider UK political landscape A metro mayor cannot simultaneously serve as an MP, a clear legal and electoral process is now underway to find someone new to take on the role Andy Burnham has won a seat as an MP - but can he still be the Mayor of Greater Manchester? No. Andy Burnham cannot be mayor and a member of parliament. He had seven days - from the moment of his election - to resign from the post which he has held for almost a decade He handed in his resignation on 19 June - triggering the process of the by-election Burnham cannot do both jobs because the Mayor of Greater Manchester holds Police and Crime Commissioner (PCC) powers and UK law strictly dictates that the office holder cannot simultaneously serve as an MP

title: Manchester's New Quiet Revolution: Why Soundproof Phone Booths are Taking Over the Workplace - About Manchester

input.lead: Manchester’s work culture has changed dramatically over the last decade.
input.summary: Manchester’s work culture has changed dramatically over the last decade. From the rise of co-working hubs in Ancoats and the Northern Quarter to tech firms filling Grade A offices in Spinningfields and MediaCity, the city has become a magnet for modern, collaborative businesses
input.evidence_text: Manchester’s work culture has changed dramatically over the last decade. From the rise of co-working hubs in Ancoats and the Northern Quarter to tech firms filling Grade A offices in Spinningfields and MediaCity, the city has become a magnet for modern, collaborative businesses. As teams grow and workplaces become more flexible, finding somewhere private to make a call, jump on a Zoom meeting or concentrate on deep work can be surprisingly hard. That’s where the humble soundproof phone booth has quietly become one of the smartest investments offices in M Open-plan offices were meant to encourage creativity, collaboration and a sense of togetherness. In many ways, they’ve done exactly that. But they’ve also introduced a few issues For businesses in Manchester’s competitive sectors – tech, media, professional services, start-ups – this isn’t just an inconvenience. It affects productivity, client relationships and even staff wellbeing Soundproof work booths are designed to solve this exact problem. They provide a small, self-contained space that blocks out surrounding noise and stops sound leaking out. That makes them ideal for Unlike traditional meeting rooms, they’re compact, flexible and can usually be installed without major building work. For landlords and office operators in central Manchester, this means they can significantly improve the usability of a floorplate without losing much lettable spa

input.evidence_text: It opened in late 2024 on the site best known in recent times for Dee Thai Share on WhatsApp Share via Email The first thing you notice is the building. Black and gold, two storeys of floor-to-ceiling glass, a canopy that sweeps upward like an ancient Chinese temple. Tang Dynasty makes an unmistakable statement on Cecil Road before you've even reached t But this is no cold monument to grandeur. The second thing you notice is the sign outside the door: A Place That Feels Like Home. And once inside, you understand exactly what they mean Tang Dynasty is no orthodox Chinese restaurant. Opening in late 2024 on the site best known in recent times for Dee Thai, it is the realisation of owner James Chen's belief that Hale needs the quality Chinese that it has arguably missed since the days of Man Zen But aside from the distinctive exterior, there are other things that set this apart from any other Chinese restaurant in the area It is, for a start, genuinely stunning inside. On both floors the mood is one of quiet luxury – golden bamboo and yellow orchids reaching upward, cream leather chairs drawn around marble tables beneath circular gold pendants

title: Heaton Hall celebrates glittering milestone on journey to reopening to the public

title: Repurposed flats will help homeless Manchester families

input.lead: A block of flats in Wythenshawe is set to become the latest property to be repurposed to provide temporary accommodation for homeless families
input.summary: A block of flats in Wythenshawe is set to become the latest property to be repurposed to provide temporary accommodation for homeless families
input.evidence_text: A block of flats in Wythenshawe is set to become the latest property to be repurposed to provide temporary accommodation for homeless families The property in Greenwood Road, Woodhouse Park, has been acquired by the Council from registered housing provider Mosscare St Vincent’s as part of wider plans to boost the city’s stock of publicly-owned, quality temporary accommodation Work is due to start this summer, and be completed in December this year, to refurbish the property as 17 self-contained flats for homeless families from Wythenshawe and south Manchester All flats will be refurbished, including new kitchens and bathrooms in each case. Two new laundry rooms for shared use will also be created, and gardens to the rear will have raised bedding for residents’ use The new accommodation will see families supported by an accommodation team based on site to help them move on as quickly as possible into permanent settled tenancies The Greenwood Road initiative is the latest example of the Council’s drive to increase its temporary accommodation stock across the city to reduce the number of out-of-area placements, helping people who become homeless to stay closer to their previous homes and support networks

input.evidence_text: A government minister has praised Rochdale borough’s award-winning approach to speech, language and communication support which has seen waiting times for families significantly reduce This recognition came as part of a visit by the Minister of State for School Standards, Georgia Gould, who learned about the innovative new methods that have been introduced locally to help families, schools and early years settings to address children’s communication needs earli Children referred for speech and language therapy previously waited up to 44 weeks for a specialist appointment due to high levels of need in the borough However, local evidence found that in many cases children could often be supported sooner if families and professionals had access to practical tools and guidance at home or in settings like the classroom In response, Rochdale Borough Council and its NHS partners have introduced a new system of early intervention, which enables families and professionals to access instant speech and language support at the touch of a button This is thanks to things like artifical intelligence (AI) technology, tailored speech and language advice through WhatsApp, a coaching platform called Help at Hand, and an online virtual noticeboard which offers practical tips, resources and activities

title: Bolton Library marks centenary of the 1926 General Strike - Bolton Council

input.lead: Bolton Library and Museum is marking the centenary of one of the largest industrial disputes in British history with a special display
input.summary: Bolton Library and Museum is marking the centenary of one of the largest industrial disputes in British history with a special display
input.evidence_text: Bolton Library and Museum is marking the centenary of one of the largest industrial disputes in British history with a special display 100 years ago, in May 1926, millions of workers across Britain took part in the General Strike The strike originated as a dispute between coal mine owners and miners over reduced wages and longer working hours When the Trades Union Congress (TUC) declared their support for the miners, other industrial workers - including those in transport, engineering, and textiles - also walked out, and it became the first ever General Strike in British history The strike was called on 3 May 1926 and lasted nine days, where the first few days of the strike saw the transport network in Bolton grind to a halt without bus and train drivers Food deliveries were delayed, and some textile mills stopped operating

title: Marina Abramović: Balkan Erotic Epic | Aviva Studios

input.lead: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović .
input.summary: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović . Regarded as the world’s most influential performance a
input.evidence_text: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović . Regarded as the world’s most influential performance artist, Abramović’s transformative work has reached millions of people – from opening the Pyramid Stage at Glastonbury Festival t A four-hour ritual where ancient myth meets performance art, Balkan Erotic Epic explores the eroticism, spirituality and traditions of Abramović’s homeland through 13 visceral scenes Brought to life by a cast of over 70 performers, dancers, musicians and singers, the experience unfolds across Aviva Studios. Audiences are free to choose their own path – with pop-up encounters punctuating the action, bringing bodies together in dance, song and ritual Watch Fertility Rite , a fevered ritual where bodies writhe against the ground in a desperate call for fertility. Witness Massaging the Breast where women gesticulate over graves to awaken the earth. In Scaring the Gods , women bare themselves to the sky to banish storms. Listen Unafraid to push the boundaries of eroticism and performance art, Balkan Erotic Epic asks its audience a timeless question: What are our bodies truly for?

title: Tchaikovsky Symphony No 6

input.summary: Mel Bonis Ophélie Op 165 Emily Dunbar New work* Sergei Rachmaninov Rhapsody on a Theme of Paganini Op 43 Piotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky Symphony No 6 in B minor Op 74 ‘Pathétique’ Clemens Schuldt, Jasper Lecon* conductors Liana Storey piano RNCM Symphony Orchestra Tchaikovsky’s ‘ Pathé
input.evidence_text: Mel Bonis Ophélie Op 165 Emily Dunbar New work* Sergei Rachmaninov Rhapsody on a Theme of Paganini Op 43 Piotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky Symphony No 6 in B minor Op 74 ‘Pathétique’ Clemens Schuldt, Jasper Lecon* conductors Liana Storey piano RNCM Symphony Orchestra Tchaikovsky’s ‘ Pathétique ’ Symphony stands as an unnerving prelude to the composer’s untimely death, which occurred just nine days after its premiere. Bleak yet epic, the true narrative behind this programmatic work is shrouded in as much mystery as the circumstances surroundin Tchaikovsky sets the tone with a haunting opening, before moving into a lilting yet unsettled waltz. The third movement creates the illusion of a triumphant conclusion, only for this hope to be undone in the final movement Perhaps a requiem to himself, the final Adagio lamentoso strays from the traditional symphonic form. Is this sombre, mournful elegy a resignation to fate?

The move marks a new chapter for the pair, who will also launch Boutikk, a neighbouring provisions store inspired by Scandinavian food culture and tr Originally opened in 2020, OSMA quickly established itself as one of Greater Manchester’s most acclaimed neighbourhood restaurants. Named after a combination of Oslo and Manchester, the restaurant blended Stoermann-Naess’ Norwegian heritage with Heron’s classical culinary trainin The return will see Heron resume her role as Executive Chef, with a menu centred around seasonal produce, fresh seafood, local meats and vegetable-led dishes. A number of OSMA’s most popular plates are expected to make a comeback, alongside the restaurant’s well-known sharing Sun While the restaurant occupies one side of the development, Boutikk will introduce a new offering to the city centre. input.evidence_text: The £35 million project set within 154 acres has transformed the former site of Worsley New Hall, restoring the historic walled garden, lake and surviving buildings Originally built for the 1st Earl of Ellesmere between 1840 and 1845, Worsley New Hall was a grand Gothic-style mansion, complete with glorious, formal landscaped gardens It’s now one of UK’s must-see gardens with so much to enjoy for visitors of all ages Step inside the magnificent 11-acre Weston Walled Garden, the site’s jewel in the crown. One of the largest Victorian walled gardens in the UK, it showcases a range of growing techniques and edible plants There’s also the spectacular Paradise Garden showcasing exotic planting inspired by Asiatic and Mediterranean gardens. The orchard and nearby bee and butterfly garden, have been designed to attract wildlife The Peel Learning Garden, supported by The Peel Group, will help schoolchildren learn how plants affect our lives and the planet, whilst the Community Wellbeing Garden provides a space for people to garden, socialise and relax

The move follows a significant decline in placements, with FE Wee To me, those two things are closely connected, for years, the conversation around skills and early careers has focused heavily on supply. More qualifications, more bootcamps, more people trained, more pathways into industry Those things matter. But creating talent is only half the equation. The other half is making sure employers have the capacity, confidence and support to absorb that talent Over the last year, Manchester Digital has been running TConnect, a pilot supported by the Gatsby Foundation and Greater Manchester Combined Authority that aimed to make it easier for employers to engage with T Level placements. title: Prolific North

input.summary: A doc created by Manchester production company, Eight Engines, telling the story of four friends who went to the 1986 World Cup in Mexico and never came home, has captivated football fans around the world
input.evidence_text: Agency Search shifts, social commerce and smarter media buying: Inside Embryo’s e-commerce unboxed event Seventy7 lands ‘perfect next step’ as it takes on Royal Doulton social brief Creative New Bradford partnership aims to tackle creative skills gap with industry-backed academy Lost Down Mexico Way follows the journey of a group of Wolves fans that travelled to Mexico on a one-way flight 40 years ago and ended up staying and embarking on new lives in the US [a narrative that seems far less likely to unfold for any fans attending Mexican games at this ye The 40-minute short film was created and filmed by Eight Engines last summer in various locations across The US and Mexico but has picked up pace this month following a stream of national attention coinciding with the 2026 World Cup starting It now has over 160,000 views on YouTube alone and is causing conversations in the media and across football forums around the world, The compelling story has been picked up by the BBC, The Times and The Telegraph, amongst others Jack Leigh, managing director of Eight Engines and the director of the film, said: “”From the first minute I heard this story, I was in love with it. It’s an odd job, trying to turn a bunch of stories four different people have been telling for years into something with a beginni “That’s the feeling I wanted to bottle. It wasn’t easy at times, but the response shows how much people have been craving this kind of content. It’s a story that shows a culture that’s often portrayed negatively but actually has so much soul.”

title: Summize makes US acquisition after £40m investment

input.lead: Manchester-based LegalTech Summize has acquired key assets and staff from Seattle-based consultancy InnoLaw Group.

input.evidence_text: Summize makes US acquisition after £40m investment Posted on June 25, 2026 by Chris Maguire Summize makes US acquisition after £40m investment Manchester-based LegalTech has acquired key assets and staff from Seattle contract lifecycle management (CLM) consultancy InnoLaw Group Share: Deals Manchester-based LegalTech Summize has acquired key assets and staff from Seattle-based consultancy InnoLaw Group. Summize’s CEO and co-founder, Tom Dunlop, said the deal represented a ‘significant step’ for the company following the completion of a £40m funding and reinvestment round at the start of the year. InnoLaw Group is a niche contract lifecycle management (CLM) consultancy that helps legal teams deploy and optimise technology at scale. The acquisition strengthens Summize’s ability to deliver complex, enterprise-grade CLM implementations to its growing global customer base. Dunlop said: “The InnoLaw team is highly respected across the legal industry because they understand the realities of in-house legal work and what it actually takes to make CLM successful and useful. Maven-backed Summize raises another £40m to grow in US “Bringing the InnoLaw team and the vast amount of knowledge and associated content in-house is a significant step for Summize. “Their depth of experience in CLM implementation and adoption is exceptional, and their focus on genuine customer value mirrors our own. “As we welcome more global customers of different sizes from multiple industry sectors, having this level of operational expertise in-house means we can guarantee the kind of value and outcomes our customers expect from AI contract intelligence.” Lara Trope, chief client officer at InnoLaw, said: “We’ve worked with in-house teams for years and have seen where traditional CLM platforms struggle wit

title: Heaton Hall shares regeneration update ahead of public reopening

input.lead: Specialist conservators have painstakingly renewed Heaton Hall’s chandeliers as the building undergoes a long-term refurbishment before reopening to the public The historic property has been undergoing work to safeguard the building for future generations, including the Orangery
input.summary: Specialist conservators have painstakingly renewed Heaton Hall’s chandeliers as the building undergoes a long-term refurbishment before reopening to the public The historic property has been undergoing work to safeguard the building for future generations, including the Orangery
input.evidence_text: Specialist conservators have painstakingly renewed Heaton Hall’s chandeliers as the building undergoes a long-term refurbishment before reopening to the public The historic property has been undergoing work to safeguard the building for future generations, including the Orangery building, which has been closed for nearly 15 years The programme to rewire Heaton Hall has been ongoing since November 2025, requiring a detailed room-by-room project plan to encase and protect the various priceless paintings, statues and furniture items, as well as doors and flooring, before any work could even begin A full renewal of the electrical systems has taken place to make sure modern fire detection and alarm systems are in place throughout the hall, while also improving security systems and electrical reliability – reducing the need for maintenance and future investment The Hall will be ‘future-proofed’ with improved Wi-Fi and data infrastructure, while exterior spotlights have been installed capable of active light projections to enliven the building for historical and cultural events The investment will also support further commercial filming and specialist cultural uses. For example, the Hall has been used as a location for recent productions The House of Guinness and A Gentleman in Moscow

The neoclassical design has made it one of the city’s most-loved buildings, holding thousands of stories in its walls and on its shelves. The square itself ha From here, the tour takes you through the city’s heart, down the colourful streets of the Gay Village and the calming Sackville Gardens. It is here that you will see the monuments that honour Manchester’s role in the fight for equality and the city’s pioneering LGBTQ+ community a This guided tour is filled with monuments and tales from many moons ago. The Medieval Quarter is the perfect place to soak in all that history. It is here where you will see the oldest public library in the English-speaking world, Chetham’s Library . Housed in a beautiful sandsto During all this walking, your guide will point out local street art, hidden corners and stories you’d easily miss on your own. Tales of suffragettes, reformers, musicians and everyday Mancunians who’ve all left their mark on the city in their own way

title: The Surge: An Ode to Sinéad O'Connor at Aviva Studios

It was emotionally unguarded in her music, and uncompromising in public – used to speak uncomfortable truths even when doing so would bring personal and professional damage. The Surge: An Ode to Sinéad O’Connor takes Conceived, choreographed and directed by the Tony Award-winning Sonya Tayeh, The Surge is the first major stage production devoted to O’Connor’s life and work, presented by New York’s Joyce Theater and Factory International . Performed by a company of 10 women spanning a wide ran “Sinéad has been a part of my life since her first album arrived blaring out car windows in Detroit, where I’m from,” Sonya Tayeh told Crack Magazine . “All of those memories came flooding back when she passed. I was heartbroken and had this sudden vision of 10 women standing in That initial image grew into a deliberate challenge to how age is typically framed in the dance world. The performers cut through the idea of ageing as something to fear, conceal or overcome. Time is treated as a privilege that carries both experience and beauty

Altrincham’s charter dates back to 1290, it’s location at the intersection of the Cheshire farm belt and the commercial commuter zone of Manchester means the town has a long and important history. Altrincham market is now at the heart of the regeneration of ‘the modern market tow The covered market hosts regional food producers, vintage fashion and furniture, home & garden and contemporary craft on a Tuesday, Friday, Saturday and Sunday Altrincham Market House Opening Hours Tues-Sat : 9am-10pm Sun : 9am-6pm Mon : Closed The Outdoor Market (Shopping) Tues: 8am-3pm Fri: 8am-3pm Sat: 8am-4pm Sun: 10am-4pm Altrincham Market House is home to award-winning independent quality food operators. title: Bolton Car Boot Sale

input.lead: Bolton Car Boot Sale at Bolton Car Boot Sale
input.summary: Huge Sunday car boot at Bolton's Macron Stadium (BL6 6JW), Feb-Dec. 300+ stalls, £1 per car entry, catering, toilets and dog-friendly Event date: 2026-06-28 Venue: Bolton Car Boot Sale Price: £1 Booking: https://carbootdirectory.co.uk/listings/bolton-car-boot/ Status: https://sch
input.evidence_text: Bolton Car Boot Sale | Bolton Sunday Car Boot Sale | Times, Prices & Info Skip to content 210+ UK car boot sales — find one near you this weekend Type your postcode or town to find sales near you Search car boot sales About This Car Boot Sale Bolton Car Boot Sale is one of the North West's biggest weekly boot sales, held in the hardstanding car park of the Macron Stadium (formerly Reebok), Burnden Way, Horwich, Bolton BL6 6JW. It runs every Sunday from February right through to December, drawing over 300 sellers and thousands of bargain hunters each week. Sellers can arrive and set up from 6:00 AM, with buyers welcomed in from 7:00 AM. Entry costs just £1 per car for buyers. Pitches for sellers are £14 for a car, £20 for a van and £3 for a trailer, with regular and corner pitches available on request. Bring exact change to keep the queue moving, and call organiser Andrew on 07470784644 for more details. Expect hundreds of stalls across the concrete hardstanding pitches, so it goes ahead in most weather. On-site you'll find a 5-star catering service, doughnuts and coffee, ice cream and desserts, a meat auction, fresh fruit and veg, plants, and retro sweet stalls. The sale is dog-friendly, has male and female toilets, ample parking, and kids' rides and bouncy castles to keep little ones entertained. Last confirmed open: 8 June 2026. title: Foreigner

input.lead: Credit: AO Arena AO Arena 11 June 2026 In 2026, Foreigner celebrate their 50th band anniversary with an exclusive 50th Anniversary tour.
input.summary: Credit: AO Arena AO Arena 11 June 2026 In 2026, Foreigner celebrate their 50th band anniversary with an exclusive 50th Anniversary tour. The tour offers a look back at five decades of rock history and unites the greatest anthems of one of the most successful classic rock bands of
input.evidence_text: Credit: AO Arena AO Arena 11 June 2026 In 2026, Foreigner celebrate their 50th band anniversary with an exclusive 50th Anniversary tour. The tour offers a look back at five decades of rock history and unites the greatest anthems of one of the most successful classic rock bands of all time. With 10 multi-platinum albums, 16 Top-30 hits, and more than 80 million records sold, Foreigner are among the most popular rock acts in the world. Songs like Cold As Ice, Juke Box Hero, Urgent, Waiting for a Girl Like You or the worldwide hit I Want To Know What Love Is are staples of the genre and an integral part of the international rock legacy. In 2024, Foreigner were inducted into the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame – another milestone in the band’s career. The setlist of the 50th Anniversary Tour spans from their early successes in the 70s , through to the legendary ‘ Album 4 ‘ – which spent more time at No. 1 on the Billboard charts than any other album in Atlantic Records’ history – to more recent hits and timeless classics. 🎟️ Find tickets here

input.evidence_text: A ghost story by Tim Foley · Directed by Neil Bettles Following a sell-out UK tour and London run, the award-winning ThickSkin present this chilling new play by Tim Foley (former Bruntwood Prize-winner) When an out-of-work actor takes a job playing a ghost at an old countryside manor, he expects nothing more than a few cheap scares. But as night after night unfolds, the role begins to blur with reality — and something far more sinister starts to emerge Part ghost story, part psychological thriller, It Walks Around The House At Night draws audiences into a world of creeping dread, unexpected twists and dark humour. A haunted house story for a new era, brought to life through inventive staging, immersive sound and bold visual eff ThickSkin, one of the UK’s most exciting touring companies, joins forces with Tim Foley (award-winning writer of Doctor Who audio dramas; Electric Rosary, Royal Exchange Theatre) to create this thrilling production. Directed by Neil Bettles (ThickSkin’s How Not To Drown, Disney’s Unsettling, darkly comic and full of surprises, the stage is set… for terror! Age Recommendation: 14+ Content Warnings: Loud and sudden noises, haze, strobe, flashing lights, occasional strong language, horror imagery and jump scares. Runtime: 90 minutes (no interval)

input.evidence_text: --> SUMMER BOAT PARTY НА ТЕМЗІ в Лондон ᐉ Билет на концерт Купить на 11 июля 2026 ᐉ mticket.uk --> --> КОНЦЕРТЫ ТУРЫ ЭКСКУРСИИ Добавить событие Условия использования Политика конфиденциальности Помощь --> --> --> --> --> --> --> --> --> --> --> --> Личный кабинет Контакты --> --> 183121-133408 Афиша Великобритания » Лондон » КОНЦЕРТЫ » Temple Pier » SUMMER BOAT PARTY НА ТЕМЗ... 65 GBP Купить билет 11 июля SUMMER BOAT PARTY НА ТЕМЗІ Лондон 65 GBP Лондон Temple Pier A3211, WC2R 2PN, Temple Купить билет Поделиться в соцсетях Заказать билеты на "SUMMER BOAT PARTY НА ТЕМЗІ" Выбрать дату 11 июл. суббота --> --> --> 19:00 Открытие дверей: 18:00 65 GBP Купить билет О событии SUMMER BOAT PARTY в Лондоне! 11 июля 2026 года в Festival Pier состоится концерт SUMMER BOAT PARTY в Лондоне. Summer Boat Party – это формат летней вечеринки на борту теплохода, курсирующего по реке Темза в самом сердце Лондона. Такие события совмещают атмосферу клубного мероприятия, речной прогулки и обзорной экскурсии по городу. Во время круиза гости любуются знаковыми панорамами столицы — от мостов и набережных до исторических зданий и современных небоскребов и параллельно наслаждаются музыкой, танцами и летним настроением. Украинская вечеринка на Темзе Summer Boat Party привлекает не только посетителей города, но и тех, кто живет в Лондоне, ведь это возможность увидеть город с необычного ракурса и провести время в формате open-air клуба на воде. Это событие станет незабываемым воспоминанием о вечере, особенно на закате, когда Темза и архитектура британской столицы приобретают особую романтическую атмосферу. Правила мероприятия: Возрастные ограничения: 18+ / Требуется удостоверение личности. БИЛЕТЫ НЕ ВОЗВРАЩАЮТСЯ: Билеты можно вернуть ТОЛЬКО в случае отмены мероприятия или переноса на другой день! Гостей ждут любимые хиты, живой звук, море эмоций и моменты, которые захочется сохранить в памяти над Важной частью творческой миссии артиста остается поддержка Украины — часть средств от концерта будет передана на помощь ВСУ! title: Innovation Office Hours with Naomi Timperley - for founders, startups, scale-ups, innovation leaders and ambitious businesses looking to grow
source_url:
https://events.compiledmcr.com/

Overview

Join Naomi for a 30-minute one-to-one for founders, startups, scale-ups, innovation leaders and ambitious businesses looking to grow.

Expert Office Hours - for founders, startups, scale-ups, innovation leaders and ambitious businesses looking to grow

Sign up for a 30-minute one-to-one at The Tech Hub at No.1 Circle Square hosted by Bruntwood SciTech.

Expert Office Hours with Naomi Timperley

Building a business can be exciting, but it can also be lonely. Sometimes what you need isn't another webinar or workshop, you need someone independent to help you sense-check your thinking, challenge assumptions, unlock opportunities and identify the next steps.

Join Naomi Timperley for a free 30-minute one-to-one session.

Naomi has spent more than 15 years supporting founders, startups, scale-ups and innovation-led organisations. She is Co-Founder of Tech North Advocates, UK Lead for Global Tech Advocates, Co-Founder of End Game and was named Computer Weekly's Most Influential Woman in UK Tech 2025.

Whether you're looking to:

• Validate a new idea or business model
• Navigate growth challenges
• Explore AI adoption and innovation opportunities
• Build strategic partnerships
• Improve your proposition and positioning
• Access funding, investment or support programmes
• Expand your network and connections
• Sense-check a key business decision
• Work out what comes next

These informal office hours are an opportunity to discuss your challenges, opportunities and ambitions and leave with practical advice, useful connections and clear next steps.

Suitable for founders, startups, scale-ups, innovation leaders and ambitious businesses looking to grow.

Use the booking form to book a slot. Time slots will be for 30 minutes.

See you there!

This Thursday Gathering with Sister explores what happens when you take the exciting momentum in Greater Manchester and link up with international partners. With a spotlight on connecting Ireland and Manchester, we’ll be discussing how to turn the growing ties between these two thriving ecosystems into the next big opportunity for local businesses.\xa0


Whether you're a founder just starting to build, an investor looking for the next breakout, or a business leader who hasn’t thought about international growth yet, this session is for you.


You’ll hear from experts from both sides of the Irish sea, including Invest Manchester, Innovation Exchange Ireland, Uniquely, Guinness Enterprise Centre, ClimbUK and Platform94 on how regional and cross-border partnerships and shared talent can fast-track your progress.\xa0

title: Bioengineering Pathways: Academia and Entrepreneurship
source_url:
https://events.compiledmcr.com/

The session will highlight how scientists can navigate careers spanning academia, industry, and entrepreneurship. Drawing on the experiences of our speakers, we will explore complementary career trajectories, including:

  • ​Transitioning across international biotech ecosystems and startup environments

  • ​Combining academic leadership with company co-founding

  • ​Bridging fundamental research with commercial application, particularly in sustainability and climate biotech

​Whether you are exploring commercialisation, developing new technologies, or considering future career directions, join us to hear inspiring stories and learn how Nucleate UK partners with Advanced Research and Invention Agency (ARIA) to accelerate ambitious ideas.

Speakers:
Lucia Burdisso, PhD - Lucia is the Chief Scientific Officer at Deep Blue Biotech, where she leads the development of a cyanobacterial synthetic biology platform for sustainable, CO₂-based chemical production. A biotechnologist with a PhD and more than 10 years of experience in R&D across academia and biotech startups, Lucia has worked in 10 laboratories across 4 countries, including startups in Argentina and the San Francisco Bay Area, and is now based in Sheffield.

Ressa Lebedinec, PhD - Ressa is a molecular mycologist and Senior Biomolecular Engineer at The University of Manchester Biotechnology Institute, with over 10 years of experience in fungal biology, biomolecular engineering, and sustainable biotechnology. She is the co-founder of mushroom+, a deep-tech platform formed in 2025 within the Carbon13 venture builder ecosystem.
mushroom+ is developing technologies that enhance the protein content and performance of mushrooms, transforming existing mushroom farms into climate-positive protein hubs. By increasing protein yield and nutritional density per kilogram of biomass, the company aims to address critical environmental and economic challenges in food production, without requiring the food system to be rebuilt from scratch.

Daniele Parisi, PhD - Daniele is a Senior Scientist at C3, where he leads efforts in scaling up fermentation processes and solving challenges related to circular biomanufacturing. With over a decade of experience in sustainability-driven research and development, he brings deep expertise in fermentation, downstream processing (DSP), and biocatalysis. Daniele holds a PhD in Molecular Biology and Biochemistry, and has contributed to both early-stage sustainability-focused startups and global companies, including his recent work with Croda.

Drinks and mexican food will be provided during the networking session with peers and potential collaborators.

title: CONEXEN Manchester July
source_url:
https://events.compiledmcr.com/

Registration needed: https:/conexen.net/conexen-events/conexen-manchester-july/

Welcome to CONEXEN Manchester – A warm and welcoming community for professionals and founders in biopharma, life sciences, and health tech in Manchester! Here, we invite you to join us for an informal and friendly gathering where connections are made, knowledge is shared, and friendships are formed. Come with an open heart and a willingness to “listen” rather than “sell.”

​​We will be hosting our Manchester event at the amazing space of Cloud 23 Cocktail Bar. It is a casual setting where we can come together, exchange greetings, engage in conversation, and simply enjoy a pleasant time.

​​Picture this as more than just an event, it’s a life sciences community gathering. Our focus is on providing you with an opportunity to meet fellow professionals and founders in a relaxed and amiable environment. We’ll share conversations over delicious food and drinks, and leave with a sense of excitement and a pocketful of new contacts and ideas.

​​Join us for an evening surrounded by incredible people, and let’s make it a night to remember. Looking forward to sharing good company and creating memorable connections!

​Theme: The Forces Reshaping Work & Culture in Life Sciences.

Join a focused discussion led by Paul Bradley-Law on the forces redefining culture in a hybrid world. Our panellists will explore how hybrid working, evolving HR trends, leadership style, and a multi-generational workforce are reshaping how organisations operate, alongside the growing influence of AI in the workplace.

We’ll examine how trust is built through clarity and candour in uncertain times—without defaulting to micromanagement—and how to address “proximity bias” to ensure fair visibility, recognition, and opportunity across distributed teams.

Join peers shaping the North-West Life Sciences ecosystem and driving the region’s growing influence.

​Moderator: Paul Bradley-Law (Founder, The Great People Machine)

​Panelists:

  • Ike Cooke, Business Growth & Commercialisation Manager, LYVA Labs

  • Julia Buckler, Senior Director, Site Head, Qiagen

​The format of the night:

  • ​18:00 Doors open – drinks, networking and meeting great people.

  • ​18:30 Talks start

  • ​19:15 Drinks, food and networking.

  • ​20:30 End of the night – you leave with new contacts and fresh ideas.

​​Venue: Cloud 23 Cocktail Bar – The Manchester Deansgate Hotel, 303 Deansgate, Manchester M3 4LQ, United Kingdom.

This event is proudly supported by Nexa Life Sciences and Kadans Science Partner, hosted by Kadans Science Partner.

​

​And finally…

​This is a ticketed event. Food and drinks for the night are included.

​Please let your colleagues and industry friends know about us, we hope to build it into a great resource for the ecosystem over the coming months and years. Attendance will be taken at this event. If you register but don’t show up, this has a knock-on effect on event arrangements. If it happens 3 times, we may need to reconsider your participation. This is simply a measure to allow appropriate respect to the organisers, speakers, sponsors, and your fellow Conexers.

​See you there!

​Ashley, Julia, and Sajid
Manchester Organisers

​Tickets FAQs: https:/www.conexen.net/faq

Questions

title: Unlocking New Possibilities with the Oracle AI Ecosystem
source_url:
https://events.compiledmcr.com/

An executive innovation experience, connecting AI, data and applications to real business outcomes.

Unlocking New Possibilities with the Oracle AI Ecosystem

Join us at Oracle's offices in Moorgate, London for Kovaion Connect 2026 - a focused executive briefing showing how Oracle Applications, Data, AI, OCI and Oracle APEX come together to help organisations innovate faster, operate smarter and unlock measurable business outcomes.

This is a half-day session built for senior decision-makers, combining thought leadership, the latest Oracle AI product updates, live demonstrations and one-to-one advisory conversations. The morning briefing moves from strategy to live innovation, followed by a networking lunch and optional private advisory slots in the afternoon. One Oracle. More value. Limitless innovation.

What you can expect

  • Executive insights into how AI, data and enterprise applications are reshaping the way organisations operate

  • The newest embedded AI capabilities across Oracle Fusion Applications

  • Release highlights and AI enhancements in Oracle APEX that accelerate enterprise innovation

  • Live demos of intelligent enterprise applications built on Oracle APEX and Oracle AI

  • A practical view of building an AI-ready data foundation with unified analytics

  • Private 1:1 executive advisory discussions with Oracle and Kovaion experts (afternoon, by request)

  • A networking lunch to exchange perspectives with peers and specialists

The day at a glance

  • 09:30 – 13:30 - Main session and networking lunch

  • 13:30 – 17:00 - Optional 1:1 executive advisory sessions

Who should attend

This briefing is designed for CIOs and CTOs, Heads of Applications, IT and Operations, Data and AI leaders, and Transformation heads - the people setting the enterprise technology, AI and digital strategy agenda.

Featured speakers include

  • Simon Hunt - Head of Product Management, Oracle APEX, Oracle UK

  • Mohana Priya Venkatachalam - Chief Projects Officer, Kovaion UK

  • Srinivas Nadella - Strategic Accounts Manager, Kovaion UK

With more Oracle and industry speakers to be announced.

You can view full agenda here - https:/www.kovaion.com/kovaion-connect-2026/

Venue: Oracle Corporation UK Ltd., One South Place, London EC2M 2RB - minutes from Moorgate, Liverpool Street and Bank stations, with rail, Underground and Elizabeth line connections.

Abstract;
In this age of AI, where AI permeates our every system. Do we need to think differently about AI. Do we need to move beyond what it CAN do to what it SHOULD do and HOW and for WHOM?

Speaker:
Stuart Coulson is the Principal Cyber Security Service Manager at a global telecoms giant, the founder of HiddenText, and someone who’s spent 20+ years turning complexity into clarity. He doesn’t do fluff. Whether mentoring start-ups or advising boardrooms, he challenges lazy thinking, strips away jargon, and leaves people with ideas they can actually use. Recognised as Cyber Mentor of the Year (2023) and a Top 50 Tech Leader (2025), Stuart’s mission is simple: build resilience, grow leaders, and make mentoring a culture that outlives him.

title: Female Founders North Summer Social - Manchester
source_url:
https://events.compiledmcr.com/

Join us on the terrace at Neo for a relaxed social evening with drinks and nibbles in our beautiful mezzanine lounge.

Female Founders North brings together digital and tech founders from across the region in a safe, supportive environment to share stories, experiences, and build meaningful connections through informal networking.

This is your opportunity to:

  • Expand your tech and digital network

  • Discuss barriers, challenges, and opportunities with fellow entrepreneurs

  • Connect with founders who have navigated similar journeys or are currently on their entrepreneurial path

  • Be part of a thriving community that's been running successfully in Leeds for over two years and recently expanded to Manchester

Bruntwood SciTech has successfully delivered Female Founders Incubators across Leeds, Manchester, and Alderley Park, helping startups grow and supporting the next generation of women-owned tech businesses.

This event is designed for:

  • Female Founders and Female Entrepreneurs

  • Female CEOs and Directors at similar levels

Allies who can support women on their entrepreneurial journey are very welcome

If you are a consultant or intermediary, please explain why you wish to attend in the additional comments box when applying for your ticket.

  • Due to venue capacity, we can only accept one person per company

  • This is a quarterly event, so don't miss this opportunity to connect with your community

Ready to expand your network and be inspired by fellow female founders

They praised the city region’s “commitments around affordability, infrastructure, industry partnership and long-term growth potential” Campbell Glennie, CEO of the TV Festival and The TV Foundation that organises it, said: “Greater Manchester presented a vision for the Festival that combined genuine creative ambition and future-facing energy with practical accessibility and affordability for delegates. title: Manchester inward investment: ‘Reasons for optimism' despite fall in FDI schemes

input.lead: Manchester inward investment: ‘Reasons for optimism’ despite fall in FDI schemes City attracted 31 FDI projects in 2025 – down from 44 in 2024 Economic Development Alistair Houghton Editor, Business Live 05:00, 23 Jun 2026 Manchester City Centre se Manchester was Britain's best-p
input.summary: Manchester inward investment: ‘Reasons for optimism’ despite fall in FDI schemes City attracted 31 FDI projects in 2025 – down from 44 in 2024 Economic Development Alistair Houghton Editor, Business Live 05:00, 23 Jun 2026 Manchester City Centre se Manchester was Britain's best-p
input.evidence_text: Manchester inward investment: ‘Reasons for optimism’ despite fall in FDI schemes City attracted 31 FDI projects in 2025 – down from 44 in 2024 Economic Development Alistair Houghton Editor, Business Live 05:00, 23 Jun 2026 Manchester City Centre se Manchester was Britain's best-performing city outside London for attracting Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) in 2025 despite a fall in the number of projects, new figures from EY have shown The latest EY 2026 UK Attractiveness Survey, which ranked 259 European regions on the number of FDI projects they attracted last year, showed Manchester attracted 31 FDI projects in 2025 – down from 44 in 2024. That made it the best-performing city outside of London for FDI for t Key FDI sectors included software and IT services, with six projects, with four projects in the health and social work sector and another four in finance, business and professional services The UK saw a total of 730 FDI projects last year, down 14% on 853 in 2024. The North West was the fourth ranked region in the UK in terms of project numbers with 51, behind Greater London (279 projects), Scotland (108 projects) and the West Midlands (68 projects) Of the UK’s nations and regions, only Greater London, Wales and Northern Ireland saw year-on-year growth in FDI projects, with the South West flat. All other regions reported declines, with the North West seeing a 41% year-on-year fall

title: Andy Burnham wins Makerfield: When will the next Greater Manchester mayor be elected now he is an MP?

input.lead: Economic Development Andy Burnham wins Makerfield: When will the next Greater Manchester mayor be elected now he is an MP?
input.summary: Economic Development Andy Burnham wins Makerfield: When will the next Greater Manchester mayor be elected now he is an MP? Huge win in by-election paves way for Labour leadership challenge Economic Development Declan Carey Local Democracy Reporter 07:33, 19 Jun 2026 Andy Burnham
input.evidence_text: Economic Development Andy Burnham wins Makerfield: When will the next Greater Manchester mayor be elected now he is an MP? Huge win in by-election paves way for Labour leadership challenge Economic Development Declan Carey Local Democracy Reporter 07:33, 19 Jun 2026 Andy Burnham Andy Burnham is heading back to Parliament after winning the Makerfield by-election The newly elected Labour MP came out on top in the race against a strong challenge from Reform UK candidate Robert Kenyon But overnight Mr Burnham secured a seismic win, with 24,927 votes placing him well ahead of Reform challenger Robert Kenyon who secured 15,696 votes. His return to Parliament is expected to pave the way for a leadership challenge against Prime Minister Keir Starmer It marks the end of weeks of campaigning in the constituency based south of Wigan, and the start of the next political race in Greater Manchester for Andy Burnham's old job as mayor Becoming an MP disqualifies Burnham from being Greater Manchester mayor, so a replacement needs to be found for the region's top political job
